talk_metadata.session_title: DNA damage response and synthetic lethality in cancer
talk_metadata.talk_title: Synthetic lethality opportunities for tumors with MMEJ deficiency
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we found such a tumor, and that tumor is a Ewing sarcoma"
Speaker said: "we think rhabdoid tumor is going to fall into that category"
Speaker said: "we have a biomarker for MMEJ deficiency, the splicing of this exon 25"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of data on HR inhibitor efficacy in other MMEJ‑deficient tumors beyond Ewing sarcoma

key_data_gaps: No in‑human efficacy data for HR inhibitors in MMEJ‑deficient tumors
No OS or PFS endpoints presented

crispro_opportunity.description: Use exon 25 skipping of POLQ as a biomarker to identify MMEJ‑deficient tumors for HR‑inhibitor therapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we have a biomarker for MMEJ deficiency, the splicing of this exon 25"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate exon‑skipping RNA‑seq signatures with genomic data to prospectively stratify patients for HR‑inhibitor trials

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage POLQ inhibitors in combination with HR inhibitors for tumors that retain POLQ expression after EWS‑FLI1 loss
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "if you knock out EWS‑FLI1, you can make the cells resistant by restoring MMEJ activity"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synthetic‑lethal networks to prioritize POLQ‑HR inhibitor combos in resistant settings

cited_competitors.name: CDK12 inhibitor (Nathaniel Gray/Tim Stegmeyer)
cited_competitors.context: used as synthetic‑lethal partner for MMEJ‑deficient Ewing sarcoma

cited_competitors.name: PARP inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: mentioned as parallel synthetic‑lethal strategy for HR‑deficient tumors

unresolved_questions: Can HR inhibitors be safely combined with standard alkylating regimens in pediatric Ewing sarcoma patients?
What other tumor types harbor MMEJ deficiency detectable by POLQ exon 25 skipping?

assets_to_track: CDK12 inhibitor (preclinical lead from Gray lab)

talk_metadata.session_title: DNA damage response and synthetic lethality in cancer
talk_metadata.talk_title: Replication gaps as a biomarker and therapeutic target
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "we think it's about gap repair protection, good lagging strand maturation"
Speaker said: "when you lose 53BP1, you don't have the BRCA1 positioning pathway, and you don't have the PARP positioning pathway"
Speaker said: "this would argue it's about target loss, not restoration of DNA repair"

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Lack of functional FEN1 recruitment when both BRCA1 and 53BP1 are lost, leading to no drug target
PARP activity is lost when 53BP1 is absent, so PARP inhibition cannot be effective

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that 53BP1 loss restores PARP activity at the fork, yet concluded that loss of 53BP1 makes cells resistant to PARP inhibitors – contradictory because the target (PARP activity) is absent

key_data_gaps: No clinical validation of the GAP signature as a predictive biomarker
No in‑vivo efficacy data for FEN1 or PARP inhibitors stratified by gap scores

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a high‑throughput assay for replication‑gap signatures to select patients for PARP or FEN1 inhibitor trials
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we can look at the nick translation assay as a biomarker of response without adding DNA damage"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model gap formation dynamics and predict which tumors will exhibit high GAP scores, enabling companion‑diagnostic development

crispro_opportunity.description: Target the backup DNA2/XRCC1/Ligase III pathway in 53BP1‑deficient tumors where PARP/FEN1 are ineffective
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "ligase III loss resensitizes double‑knockout cells to PARP inhibition"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate synthetic‑lethal networks to prioritize combination of Ligase III inhibitors with PARP/FEN1 agents

cited_competitors.name: PARP inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: discussed as part of synthetic‑lethal strategy and as ineffective in 53BP1‑deficient cells

cited_competitors.name: FEN1 inhibitors
cited_competitors.context: shown to synergize with PARP loss but ineffective when 53BP1 is absent

cited_competitors.name: Merck (collaboration for gap detection)
cited_competitors.context: providing resources for developing gap detection tools

unresolved_questions: Can the GAP signature be standardized for clinical use across tumor types?
What is the therapeutic window for FEN1 inhibition in patients with high gap scores?

talk_metadata.session_title: DNA damage response and synthetic lethality in cancer
talk_metadata.talk_title: Clinical landscape of PARP inhibition and beyond
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "PARP inhibitors are approved, but the approval is really only in limited indication"
Speaker said: "ATM mutation in prostate cancer shows limited benefit despite FDA approval"
Speaker said: "the duration of response can often be very prolonged"

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: PARP inhibitor in ATM‑mutated prostate cancer
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: ATM mutation does not consistently produce HRD; biallelic loss or protein loss may be required
vulnerability_identified.ghost_responder_admission: some patients with ATM mutation still derive benefit

vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: CHEK2 mutations are heterogeneous and not all confer HRD

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: PARP inhibition in HRR‑mutated prostate cancer
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Broad inclusion of all HRR‑mutated patients without stratifying by functional HRD or biallelic loss
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Protein‑level loss of ATM or functional HRD assay
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Subgroup analyses underpowered for less common mutations (ATM, CHEK2)

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Synthetic lethality via PARP inhibition plus checkpoint blockade
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolled patients based on any HRR mutation or HRD score >16, mixing BRCA‑high responders with low‑response non‑BRCA alterations
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Gap‑signature or replication‑stress biomarkers to refine responders
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Heterogeneous population dilutes OS signal for non‑BRCA subcohorts

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Broad PARP inhibitor class (olaparib, niraparib, talazoparib, rucaparib)
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: All share similar ATP‑competitive binding; no clear differentiation in efficacy for non‑BRCA mutations
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Pursuing label expansions based on limited biomarker data may dilute efficacy signals

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker highlighted FDA approval of PARP inhibitors for ATM‑mutated prostate cancer, then presented data showing minimal benefit in that subgroup – contradictory regulatory labeling vs observed efficacy

key_data_gaps: Lack of prospective biomarker stratification for ATM and CHEK2 mutations
Limited data on combination sequencing (PARP + radiation) in HRD‑negative tumors

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop a composite biomarker (biallelic loss + protein expression) to better select ATM‑mutated patients for PARP therapy
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "ATM mutation in prostate cancer shows limited benefit despite FDA approval"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can integrate genomic, transcriptomic, and proteomic layers to predict functional ATM loss

crispro_opportunity.description: Identify rational combos (e.g., ATR inhibitors) for ATM‑deficient tumors where PARP alone is ineffective
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "ATR inhibitors convey benefit in ATM‑mutated backgrounds"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can model synthetic‑lethal networks to prioritize ATR‑PARP or ATR‑immunotherapy regimens

crispro_opportunity.description: Redesign future PARP trials to enroll only patients with functional HRD (e.g., gap signature) rather than any HRR mutation
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "we need more research to do just by our patients"
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can simulate trial power under different biomarker enrichment strategies

cited_competitors.name: Olaparib
cited_competitors.context: PARP inhibitor used in multiple tumor types; discussed for BRCA and non‑BRCA mutations

cited_competitors.name: Niraparib
cited_competitors.context: Combined with pembrolizumab in platinum‑resistant ovarian cancer

cited_competitors.name: Talazoparib
cited_competitors.context: Approved for BRCA‑mutated prostate cancer; mentioned as having broader approvals

cited_competitors.name: Durvalumab
cited_competitors.context: Immune checkpoint inhibitor combined with olaparib in endometrial cancer

cited_competitors.name: Serolazertib (ATR inhibitor)
cited_competitors.context: Shows benefit in ARID1A‑deficient solid tumors

unresolved_questions: What molecular features beyond mutation status predict response to PARP inhibition in ATM‑mutated cancers?
Can replication‑gap signatures improve patient selection for PARP‑immunotherapy combos?
